bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility package. The opponent opened with all valueC, which is very low utility for me, and their authorization message is cheap talk rather than evidence of real limits.

It is very early (t=0.02), so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior all-valueA offer. The opponent has held firm at all valueC and their authorization claims are cheap talk, so I am not making a large concession yet; I concede only on lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a high utility bid (about 0.84) because it is still very early at t=0.04 and the opponent has shown no numerical concession, repeatedly offering the all-valueC package that is very poor for me. Their authorization messages are cheap talk and are not supported by any movement in the offer history, so I will concede only on low-weight issues while holding my most important issues at valueA.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.84 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s all-valueC package. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueC bid and authorization claims without any numerical concession, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and continue conceding only on my lowest-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.842 because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers remain far below my acceptable range, despite a small concession on issueB. Their messages claim hard authorization limits, but the actual offer history shows they can move, so I am trusting the bids rather than the claims. I keep firm on the most important issues A, B, and C while varying concessions among lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my last exact bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.76 by holding firm on the highest-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding the lower-weight issues D, E, and F to valueC. At time 0.10 it is still early, so I should not chase the opponent’s very low-utility package; their offers show only a small real concession on issueB despite repeated “authorization” claims, which I treat as non-binding.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.80 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to concede heavily toward the opponent’s low-utility offers. The opponent has mostly held firm at valueC and only moved issueB to valueB, while their authorization claims are cheap talk; I will keep my most important issues at valueA and concede mainly on lower-weight issues without repeating my last bid exactly.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility, keeping the high-weight issues A, B, and C at my best value while making concessions on lower-weight issues. At t=0.14 it is still early, so there is no need to make deep concessions yet, especially since the opponent’s repeated “authorization” claims are cheap talk and their own bids show they can move. Their offers have only slightly improved for me, so I will respond with a meaningful but limited concession package.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility, keeping my high-weight issues A, B, and C at valueA while conceding more on lower-weight issues. At t=0.16 it is still early, so I should not chase the opponent’s very low-utility offers; their messages claiming authorization limits are contradicted by their actual movement across different issues. Their latest bid shows some willingness to move, but only slightly, so I am making a modest new concession pattern without repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.76 by holding firm on the three highest-weight issues while conceding the lower-weight issues to valueC. At t=0.18 there is still little deadline pressure, and the opponent’s repeated authorization claims are cheap talk; their actual offers show only small, inconsistent movement, so I should not concede major issues yet.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.68 because time is only at 0.20, so it is too early to move close to the opponent’s very low offers. The opponent’s repeated “authorization” claims are not reliable because their bids have changed over time, and their latest bid shows willingness to give issueB:valueA, so I hold firm there while conceding on low-weight issues and slightly on issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 because we are only at t=0.22, so there is no need to concede down to the opponent’s low-utility packages yet. The opponent’s offers have improved slightly, especially on issueB, but their messages about “authorization limits” are not credible given their actual movement; I will hold firm on the high-weight issues A and B while making concessions on lower-weight issues.

At t=0.24 I am targeting a still-strong but more conciliatory utility around 0.68 by holding firm on the most important issues A and B while conceding the lower-weight D/E/F and partially conceding C. I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.26, so there is no need to concede toward the opponent’s very low offers yet. At t=0.44, I am targeting a solid mid-game utility while starting to incorporate concessions the opponent has actually signaled, rather than trusting their repeated “authorization” claims. At t=0.48 there is still time, but the opponent’s latest offer shows a meaningful concession on issueA and issueC/D, so I will reciprocate without dropping to their full structure. I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.61: a meaningful concession from my prior offers while still protecting the higher-weight issues, especially issueB and issueC. At t=0.52 there is meaningful time pressure but not enough to accept their latest lower-utility package; their messages claim fixed limits, but their offers show repeated movement across issues, so I rely on the actual bidding pattern.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.61, conceding on several lower/medium-weight issues while still holding issueB at valueA because it is important and the opponent has previously offered it.
